title: Ensure JUnit Rules Are Used in Tests
description: JUnit rules are classes that enhance JUnit tests with additional functionalities. However, having unused JUnit rules may cause confusion, make tests harder to maintain, and slow down the test suite. This rule raises an issue when a TestRule is declared in a Test class but isn't referenced by any method.
recommendation: Ensure all declared JUnit rules in a Test class are being referenced by at least one test method. Remove any unused JUnit rules to improve code readability and maintainability.
impact: Unused JUnit rules can slow down the test suite and complicate code maintenance. Ensuring all JUnit rules are used enhances test efficiency and code quality.

checks.message: Ensure all declared JUnit rules in a Test class are being referenced by at least one test method. Remove any unused JUnit rules to improve code readability and maintainability.
